messages.subject: Corruption
messages.snippet: View this post on the web at https://ossoff.substack.com/p/corruption See, I get why some folks voted for Trump. He promised to attack a broken system. And it’s a ripe target. Even before Trump came on the scene, America had one of the most
messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://ossoff.substack.com/p/corruption
See, I get why some folks voted for Trump.
He promised to attack a broken system. And it’s a ripe target.
Even before Trump came on the scene, America had one of the most corrupt political systems in the Western world.
It’s been running on corporate money, secret money, billionaire money.
And it’s worse than ever now.
Citizens United was the worst court decision in modern American history.
When members of Congress aren’t begging for money from Washington lobbyists, they’re trying to dodge getting carpet-bombed by super PACs.
Senators get threatened with millions of dollars of attack ads over the votes they take.
And see, that is why things don’t work for ordinary people.
It’s because the people’s elected representatives don’t represent the people. They represent the big donors!
That corruption is why they defunded nursing homes to cut taxes for the rich.
Corruption is why you pay a fortune for prescriptions.
Corruption is why your insurance claim keeps getting denied.
Corruption is why hedge funds get to buy up all the houses in your neighborhood, and then your corporate landlord ignores your calls during a gas leak.
Corruption is why that ambulance costs $3,000 after you just had to get your choking toddler to the hospital.
Yes, the system really is rigged.
But Trump’s not unrigging it. He’s re-rigging it for himself!
He’s a crook and a con man, and he wants to be a king.
You can’t claim to be for ordinary men and women when you defund their hospitals and nursing homes.
You can’t claim to be for ordinary men and women when you defund school lunch programs to help feed little boys and little girls whose hard-working parents are struggling to fill the fridge.
You can’t claim to be for ordinary men and women when you do these things so you can cut taxes for the richest people in the country while you and your family business rake in massive sums from around the world.
Donald Trump’s second term has already done unfathomable damage to our country and the lives of millions of people.
And the people are beginning to see it.
But only by regaining Congressional power can we stop him.
Really.
The upcoming 2026 midterms are our last chance to restore checks and balances.
And my Senate race in Georgia is the single most critical contest: I’m the only Democratic Senator up for reelection in 2026 in a state Trump carried in 2024.
So if you’re outraged by what’s happening to our country — and you’re

messages.subject: Our first Fellowship Outcomes Report 📊
messages.snippet: For nearly 5 years, people have asked us some version of the same question: **Does the Climatebase Fellowship actually help people land new jobs?** Today, we’re answering that question—with data. Our **[2026 Climatebase Fellowship Career Ou
messages.text:
For nearly 5 years, people have asked us some version of the same question:
**Does the Climatebase Fellowship actually help people land new jobs?**
Today, we’re answering that question—with data.
Our **[2026 Climatebase Fellowship Career Outcomes Report](https://job-outcomes.climatebase.org)** — the first one we’ve ever published—is now live.
One important point upfront: the Fellowship is **_not_** just for job seekers. Only about half of Fellows join with the primary goal of landing a new role. Others come to build their network, work on startups and projects, deepen their climate knowledge, or learn from experts and leaders.
**This report focuses on the Fellows who did set out to make a career move.** We examined the outcomes of Fellows from our first six cohorts—far enough back to see what actually happened in their careers.
**Four numbers tell the short version:**
“📈== After graduating, Fellows are ====**2x more likely **====to work in climate / sustainability ==
==🌍 ====**1,110+ alumni**==== from those first six cohorts are now in climate roles==
🏢== They work at ====**250+ climate organizations**==
🤝== ====**1 in 3**==== joined an org where a Fellow already worked==“ —
That last finding says a lot about how hiring in this space really works: personal connections matter.
The full report — which you can _[read here](https://job-outcomes.climatebase.org)_ — also covers examples of where Fellows actually land, the data on how climate hiring _really_ works (there's one stat in there we can't stop citing), and success stories from Fellows in their own words. A few examples:
“"I joined to get clarity on the climate tech landscape... what I didn't expect was how powerful the network would be. I connected with a Fellow who was already at EVgo. They referred me — and that referral is what got me in the door."
— **Declan McDonnell, Senior Data Scientist at EVgo | Climatebase Fellow **
"The Climatebase Fellowship is one of the best things I've ever done. It has been utterly transformative for my life and my career."
— **Anshu Pandit, Communications at Mermaid Energy | Climatebase Fellow**** **
"After building my career in big tech, I'm thrilled to be channeling everything I've learned about scaling ideas into something that really matters — climate."
— **Marlous Tyrrell, Strategy & Growth at Mission Zero | Climatebase Fellow **“ —
The report is a five-minute read, and worth all five.
**Read the 2026 Outcomes Report →** (https://job-outcomes.climatebase.org)

messages.snippet: Slack Organization Name: Bay Area Makerfarm Slack Organization URL: https://bayareamakerfarm.slack.com/ Slack Organization Owner Email Address: julia.hasty@gmail.com We are resending the original email sent to you in May as a reminder of a
messages.text:
Slack Organization Name: Bay Area Makerfarm
Slack Organization URL: https://bayareamakerfarm.slack.com/
Slack Organization Owner Email Address: julia.hasty@gmail.com
We are resending the original email sent to you in May as a reminder of a potential action required for your Slack support data and FedRAMP. If you have already taken action to submit your declaration or made the decision you don't require FedRAMP for your Support data, please ignore this email.
Potential Action Required: If your organization requires your Slack customer support data to be stored and managed in a FedRAMP authorized system, you will need to let us know by August 19, 2026.
Why? As part of our goal to enhance and standardize the Slack customer experience, we are working towards managing Slack customer support data (e.g., support cases, case-related data and communications) in Salesforce systems that are not within a FedRAMP authorized boundary. This paves the way for Success Plans for Slack [https://www.salesforce.com/services/success-plans/].
What is FedRAMP? FedRAMP, or the Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program, is a US government program that provides a standardized approach to security and risk assessment for cloud products and services. FedRAMP compliance is a mandatory requirement for US government agencies and is sometimes necessary for companies that work with them and handle government data in a cloud environment.
What this means for you:
- If you do not require FedRAMP for your support data, no action is required. Your support data will be managed in a Salesforce support system that, although not within a FedRAMP boundary, continues to uphold our highest Trust requirements with comprehensive security controls designed to ensure the continued protection and integrity of your support data. This will enable you to access Success Plans as they launch. (Note that this change is limited to support data and does not include Customer Data that is submitted to your Slack org.) You will be notified before this change occurs.
- If FedRAMP is required for your support data, action is required. Your Primary Owner must notify us before August 19, 2026 by submitting the request described in the Help Center FAQ [https://slack.com/help/articles/50626413139859]. Your support data will remain in a system that is within a FedRAMP authorized boundary, and availability of future support innovations may be delayed while we ensure full compliance with FedRAMP requirements.

They want you to panic.
Bank Alert Scam
What you see: “Fraud Alert: Did you make a $499 purchase? Verify immediately.”
What you think: “I need to stop this right now.”
Pause if you notice:
It creates fear or urgency.
It wants you to click or call immediately.
It comes from an unexpected number.
They want you to trust them by pretending to be a local government agency or other organization.
Payment Due Scam
What you see: “You have an unpaid toll. Pay within 24 hours.”
What you think: “I’d better pay before I get a penalty.”
Pause if you notice:
You weren’t expecting a bill.
The link doesn’t match the organization.
You’re pressured to pay immediately.
They start small. Then ask for more.
“Wrong Number” Scam
What you see: “Hi! Is this John?”
What you think: “They’re just texting the wrong person.”
Pause if you notice:
They keep the conversation going.
They become unusually friendly.
They start talking about investments or easy money.
A simple pause may save you money. The STOP method can help protect you.
These steps can help you stay ahead of scammers.
Stay cautious. Always be on alert when someone asks for information or money.
Take your time. Scammers want to rush you. Slow down and think before you act.
Only trust verified sources. Double-check information through trusted channels.
Protect your info. Never share personal or financial details unless you're sure who you're dealing with.

messages.snippet: View this post on the web at https://www.acquiredbriefing.com/p/the-playbook-lessons-from-200-company Kyle’s Rating: 10/10 A curated “greatest hits” from 200+ episodes. It’s a fun departure from the usual format. Ben and David’s chemistry s
messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://www.acquiredbriefing.com/p/the-playbook-lessons-from-200-company
Kyle’s Rating: 10/10
A curated “greatest hits” from 200+ episodes. It’s a fun departure from the usual format. Ben and David’s chemistry shines as they distill hard-won insights from Sony to Oprah, perfect for both longtime fans and newcomers wanting the playbook distilled. It’s less a traditional episode and more a celebratory masterclass—like a live concert of Acquired’s best riffs.
Did an Acquired fan share this with you? Subscribe below
Ben and David distilled 12 key lessons from over 200 Acquired episodes after months of research, drawing from iconic company stories like Sony, NVIDIA, and Oprah for a special talk at Capital Camp.
1. Optimism Always Wins
Ben and David argue that optimism is the only rational stance for building world-changing companies, even in the bleakest conditions. In 1946 Japan, with GDP per capita at $17, half of Tokyo homeless, and no tech infrastructure, Akio Morita and Masaru Ibuka founded Sony with a wooden rice cooker, no market, and no military contracts. Their audacity built an icon that reshaped global consumer electronics and inspired Steve Jobs, who later said, “No Sony, no iPhone.” The insight: headwinds are real, but only optimists drive progress. Investors who back vision over macro noise capture outsized returns because the future is built by those who refuse to accept the present as permanent.
2. The Mike Moritz Corollary to Moore’s Law
Moritz realized that Moore’s Law, 10X compute every ~7 years, perpetually expands addressable markets by slashing costs. In 1990, a $2,000 486 PC reached 42% of Americans; today, a $200 smartphone with a million times the power serves over 6 billion globally. This enabled Sequoia to invest in Google, WhatsApp, Airbnb, and ByteDance. Log-scale charts show progress always feels like “nothing, then everything,” masking compounding. NVIDIA’s GPU leaps prove the law still holds. The takeaway: tech outcomes keep scaling as long as compute improves exponentially. Macro drawdowns are noise, and betting on the next wave is the only long-term play.
3. Let Your Winners Ride
Sequoia’s 1978 Apple exit, selling for 40X after 18 months, was history’s costliest mistake, missing trillions. Amazon’s post-IPO chart proves the point: 13 years from IPO yielded 10X, but holding to 2022 delivered 170X, with 99.98% of gains post-public. Growth duration, not annual rate, defines value. messages.snippet: Hello Selkies, My golly, it’s been almost two months since our epic party at Bort Meadow. Thank you so much to all of the fabulous people who made it happen! Here’s a lovely Group Photo Album from the event. Don’t hesitate to add photos (Pl
messages.text:
Hello Selkies,
My golly, it’s been almost two months since our epic party at Bort Meadow.
Thank you so much to all of the fabulous people who made it happen!
Here’s a lovely Group Photo Album
<https://photos.app.goo.gl/fHNvmPsbZ3rr2nMbA> from the event. Don’t
hesitate to add photos (Please request access if needed). Jeremie Fremaux
has offered his Photos
<https://fremauxphotography.shootproof.com/gallery/260605-selkiefest/>
including pictures from the hecka fun Photo Booth he set up…and Anka also
has a fine collection of photo
<https://www.flickr.com/gp/ankakarewicz/fMs235km23>s. Let the reminiscing
begin…
We have a full recap and update further down in this email, but first, mark
your calendar for our next event:
Cowotey V (!) this October 22-26 at Dairy Glen Campground, Coyote Hills
Regional Park, Fremont. This will be our 5th Selkie event at this beloved
location, where we plan to book less bands and leave a bit more time for
adventure, musical improv and happy chaos.
Without the fabulous musicians who play, it wouldn’t be Selkie… So now we
invite you to revisit (or peruse for the first time) the eclectic lineup of
Selkie ‘26! Please follow these folks on youtube, spotify, IG, FB...More
followers helps bands get better gigs. We also recommend Bandcamp as a way
to purchase digital or physical merch from the bands you love. THANK YOU
EPIC BANDS, WE LOVE YOU!!! And we’ll see you at the shows listed here:
[image: ✨] [image: 🔸] BaNDs wHo PlAyed SELKiE '26 [image: 🔸][image: ✨]

messages.subject: The 7 Mistakes That Kill AI Products — And How to Avoid Them (July 31)
messages.snippet: View this post on the web at https://bayareafoundersclub.substack.com/p/the-7-mistakes-that-kill-ai-products Hello, BFC Fam! 👋 The AI landscape is moving faster than ever. Over the past two weeks, we’ve seen a clear trend across the indust
messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://bayareafoundersclub.substack.com/p/the-7-mistakes-that-kill-ai-products
Hello, BFC Fam! 👋
The AI landscape is moving faster than ever.
Over the past two weeks, we’ve seen a clear trend across the industry: companies are no longer asking whether to adopt AI—they’re racing to figure out how to build better AI products, faster.
Leading AI labs continue to release more capable models at an unprecedented pace, while startups are increasingly competing on execution rather than access to technology. At the same time, AI-assisted coding has become a core part of software development, enabling much smaller teams to build products that previously required much larger engineering organizations.
For founders, the challenge is no longer just building an AI application. It’s building the right product, choosing the right go-to-market strategy, and avoiding the mistakes that cause promising AI startups to stall.
To adapt to the evolving tech landscape, we're honored to invite Alex Dang [ https://substack.com/redirect/abe37ac1-6351-45e7-8377-9c82008b4c92?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ] (Stanford Alumnus, Former Amazon Executive, McKinsey Advisor, and Co-Author of The Venture Mindset [ https://substack.com/redirect/23ee4b75-8b5c-40f0-9551-6f7b62e86acb?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ]) to lead an immersive, hands-on workshop for BFC founders. This session is designed to help you build successful AI products and steer clear of the critical pitfalls that can hinder your progress.
If you’re a founder or founder to be in the Bay Area, you’re warmly invited to join our workshop on Friday, July 31.
Hosted by: Bay Area Founders Club
Location: Zoom
Time: Friday, July 31, 2026
Register: https://luma.com/4baf44fy?coupon=BFC15
Coupon: BFC15 (for 15% off)’
Why This Workshop Matters
​Everyone’s building AI products and services, but most products still fail. Why? It’s not the tech. It’s the product thinking.
​Join Alex Dang [ https://substack.com/redirect/f3ad585f-c1b4-4889-a094-f5841aae131b?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ], Silicon Valley strategist and co-author of the global bestseller The Venture Mindset, for a no-fluff, high-impact workshop on what really separates winning AI products from the ones that never ship—or worse, launch and flop.
​You’ll learn the 7 deadly mistakes that kill AI projects—and how to avoid them using the same tools

messages.subject: How to end the redistricting wars for good
messages.snippet: View this post on the web at https://www.ifyoucankeepit.org/p/how-to-end-the-redistricting-wars In the 2024 elections, only about four percent [ https://substack.com/redirect/77bcabdf-8622-4134-8b80-f161a67720b6?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAt
messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://www.ifyoucankeepit.org/p/how-to-end-the-redistricting-wars
In the 2024 elections, only about four percent [ https://substack.com/redirect/77bcabdf-8622-4134-8b80-f161a67720b6?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ] of U.S. House seats changed hands from one party to another. Four percent. It doesn’t seem possible to make our political system even more rigid. But the two major parties are trying anyway.
In advance of the 2026 midterms, state legislators are racing to redraw congressional maps to make seats safer for one party or the other. To make matters worse, the Supreme Court’s recent decision in Louisiana v. Callais [ https://substack.com/redirect/44f4ce39-4b21-4a96-b163-3a18bee938af?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ] effectively gutted Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act — the provision that for decades allowed voters of color to challenge maps drawn to dilute their voting power.
Most Americans live in districts where the outcome was decided long before Election Day by politicians drawing lines to protect their seats. This November, millions of voters will cast ballots that are powerless to affect those outcomes. With this latest wave of mid-cycle redistricting, we’re entering a new phase of a problem that’s plagued American democracy from nearly the beginning.
It’s also a problem that most other democracies have managed to solve.
Gerrymandering persists because single-member districts allow it
In 1968, the political scientist Robert Dixon quipped that “all districting is gerrymandering.” Dixon was making an observation about a fundamental feature — and flaw — of the U.S. electoral system itself: our use of single-member districts.
Single-member districts are uniquely vulnerable to manipulation. Gerrymandering works by exploiting a common feature of them known as “wasted votes.” When a candidate wins with 60% of the vote, the 40% cast for other candidates doesn’t help to elect anyone; those votes are considered “wasted.” So, too, are the 10% the prevailing candidate earned beyond what was needed to win. Gerrymanderers are essentially engineers of this waste, designing districts to maximize the opposition’s wasted votes and minimize their own.
Read more — How to end gerrymandering [ https://substack.com/redirect/1bf073bf-6fce-4d60-8dca-15bb5c7b9221?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ]
Take Austin, Texas, as an example. In 2011

messages.text:
This event has been updated
Changed: description
Tiff and Dan's Going away party
Saturday Jul 25, 2026 ⋅ 2pm – 6pm
Pacific Time - Los Angeles
Location
Jackson Playground Park, 1645 17th St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA
https://www.google.com/maps/search/Jackson+Playground+Park,+1645+17th+St,+San+Francisco,+CA+94107,+USA?hl=en
Join with Google Meet
https://meet.google.com/vkb-uegf-mgh?hs=224
UPDATE: Thee Parkside closed on July 5th. So come to the party!!
Hopefully you also saw the email. If not, here it is:
You probably all know: Dan and I are about to move to Mexico! We leave for Oaxaca on August 1st. We are having a going away party!! We would really like to see y'all one last time before we leave. Give us a good sendoff.
Date/Time: Saturday July 25th, 2-6pm
Location: Jackson Playground Park SF: 1645 17th Ave. In the Potrero, conveniently located across the street from Thee Parkside.
We have two picnic tables reserved behind the baseball diamond.
Partners and offspring welcome. If you want to bring additional friends, send me a quick email to let me know.
Activities: these will happen on the early side.
1) Pinatas! We are hand-making some SF-themed pinatas that will have special prizes in them.
2) Small Change Auction: bid on our fancy leftover booze mixers and other weird amazing stuff for pennies! (We accept Venmo)
Food: We will provide some decent quality eats and snacks- like a hearty salad you can eat and feel morally good about yourself. Trader Joe's snacks will be heavily represented. We will include some veg, vegan and GF options. Feel free to bring some food!
Drinks: We will provide some leaded and unleaded spritzers and some A and NA canned beverages. You can bring some of your own, but if you do, keep it inconspicuous. Our park permit says no alcohol.
After 6pm, we may stagger over to Thee Parkside, and you may be able to catch us there. By then I might be a little drunk, prone to excessive self-disclosure, and possibly funnoying. We won't last long, so don't count on an evening encounter.
Want to give us a going away present? Give us some high SPF sunscreen. Partial bottles accepted.
We can't wait to see you!!
Organizer

messages.subject: Vultr - VPS Maintenance Reboot | ALRT-B2SBYMK
messages.snippet: Dear Julia Hasty, We are notifying you about a new alert as it relates to your Vultr.com account. Event Summary: VPS Maintenance Reboot Dear Customer, A critical Linux KVM vulnerability affecting virtualized environments has been disclosed.
messages.text:
Dear Julia Hasty,
We are notifying you about a new alert as it relates to your Vultr.com account.
Event Summary: VPS Maintenance Reboot
Dear Customer,
A critical Linux KVM vulnerability affecting virtualized environments has been disclosed. To protect your workloads, our security team is applying patches across our infrastructure, which requires a one-time reboot of your virtual machine. Please note that this reboot must be conducted by our team. Rebooting yourself will not avoid our scheduled reboot.
Because of the severity, this window cannot be rescheduled, and affected VMs cannot be migrated to avoid the reboot.
**Maintenance window**
Silicon Valley
Start: 2026-07-28 12:15:00 UTC
End: 2026-07-28 13:15:00 UTC
**What to expect**
Each VM will reboot once during this window. Actual interruption should be brief (~2–5 minutes) while the VM restarts — not the full hour.
**Action required**
None. Reboots are automatic. If a VM does not return to normal service after the maintenance window, please contact support through opening a ticket console.vultr.com
Impacted Instances:
2048.00 MB Regular Cloud Compute - 149.28.202.199 (Feedback)
To view additional details and updates regarding this alert, please view notifications for your account at:
https://console.vultr.com/#status
Alert Reference: ALRT-B2SBYMK
-- Vultr Support Team --

messages.subject: Do Data Centers Cause... Indian Immigration?
messages.snippet: View this post on the web at https://www.cremieux.xyz/p/do-data-centers-cause-indian-immigration Today’s post is brought to you by my sponsor, Mechanize [ https://substack.com/redirect/9cd521d7-7784-4f54-948c-befbff05dc04?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFh
messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://www.cremieux.xyz/p/do-data-centers-cause-indian-immigration
Today’s post is brought to you by my sponsor, Mechanize [ https://substack.com/redirect/9cd521d7-7784-4f54-948c-befbff05dc04?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ]. They’re hiring junior software engineers [ https://substack.com/redirect/ec34e2d1-a2ae-45e4-ad27-7a6b4a56d530?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ] at $300K/year base salary.
If you talk to a member of the public about data centers, chances are, they’re opposed. The most common arguments in opposition also generally have a left-wing character, focused on the environment or the displacement of our precious farmlands and natural resources. For a quick overview, see my plot of Gallup’s latest polling:
The other common arguments are usually uninformed or silly, but also nonpolitical: opposition to noisy buildings, disdain for having to pay higher taxes, a fear of AI replacing human workers, and so on. Few of the arguments are solidly right-wing by any measure. But one argument did recently come across my feed, and while I don’t think it’s going to become a nationally prominent one, I think it could become prominent online, and from there, it would generate headaches and acrimony for everyone involved in the discourse: the claim is that data centers “attract a segment of the tech workforce that is overwhelmingly foreign, mostly Indian. [ https://substack.com/redirect/756d9557-de73-4bf9-ab69-2582bd5efa12?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ]”
This is an interesting theory, because it’s very obviously untrue if you’ve ever been in a data center. Data centers employ few people [ https://substack.com/redirect/3389e3b3-5337-4453-932c-49fa20651fe2?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ] (~50 for a 250,000 square-foot data center is normal), about half of the employees are contractors, and basically none of them are H-1B workers. In fact, if we look at Census data on the demographics of the people working in data centers, the whole idea looks preposterous:
For most people, this is probably enough evidence to conclude that the claim about data centers attracting “a segment of the tech workforce that is overwhelmingly foreign, mostly Indian” is false, but there’s a way to defend the claim. One could argue that the alleged influx of Indians is due to the emergence of “the secondary ecosystem of clou

messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://bayareafoundersclub.substack.com/p/from-zero-to-10m-the-silicon-valley-a3a
Building a great product is only half the battle. The hardest part is getting it into the hands of customers.
​In this exclusive four-part masterclass, Chang Chen, founder of Hockey Stick Growth, shares the same go-to-market frameworks his team has used with 100+ AI and technology companies, generating 35B+ impressionsover the past year.
​Unlike traditional lectures, every session is designed as a hands-on workshop where you’ll apply the frameworks directly to your own startup and leave with an actionable GTM plan.
​Collectively generating over 35B+ impressions and collaborating with 20,000+ creators across campaigns.
How She Scaled an AI App to 3M Visitors/Mo Using AI SEO ft Chang Chen
What You’ll Learn
​Session 1 — Product-Market Fit & Product-Channel Fit
​Learn how to determine whether you’ve truly achieved product-market fit—and identify the marketing channels that naturally match your product before spending on growth.
​You’ll learn:
​PMF vs. Brand-Market Fit
​Product-Channel Fit
​Finding your growth wedge
​PLG vs. PLS vs. SLG
​Evaluating 20+ acquisition channels
​Session 2 — KOL & Creator-Led Growth
​Learn how today’s fastest-growing startups partner with creators to build movements instead of simply buying sponsorships.
​Topics include:
​Creator sourcing framework
​Campaign briefing process
​Platform selection
​Budget allocation
​Performance measurement
​2026 creator marketing trends
​Session 3 — SEO & GEO (Generative Engine Optimization)
​Search is changing.
​Discover how startups can rank not only on Google—but also become cited inside ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini, and other AI search engines.
​Topics include:
​Modern SEO fundamentals
​GEO strategies
​AI citation optimization
​Content that compounds over time
​UGC as a growth engine
​Session 4 — B2B Go-To-Market
​Selling to enterprises requires an entirely different playbook.
​This session covers:
​Product-led Sales
​Sales-led Growth
​Enterprise positioning
​Technical credibility
​Buying committee navigation
​Building your first enterprise pipeline
​📅 Schedule
​This is a 4-part live masterclass delivered every other week, giving you time to apply what you learn between sessions.

Session 2: August 22 • 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM (PT)
Session 3: September 5 • 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM (PT)
